untranslatable_reason: This signal requires calculation of fair roll value based on cost-of-carry formula (S × (t/12) × r), which involves: (1) underlying price S, (2) time period in months, and (3) interest rate r. The allowed indicators do not include direct access to: underlying asset price, contract expiration dates, interest rates, or synthetic spread pricing. Additionally, the signal requires comparing actual roll prices against calculated fair values with dynamic thresholds—this requires multi-leg derivative pricing that is outside the scope of single-instrument OHLCV technical indicators.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_108_0 is a calendar roll valuation strategy from options theory. It cannot be meaningfully translated into a technical indicator-based rule without: (1) explicit interest rate data input, (2) contract-specific expiration metadata, (3) multi-leg spread pricing logic, and (4) cost-of-carry calculations. This is a fundamental derivatives pricing model, not a price-action technical pattern. Recommend implementing as a custom module in backtest engine with dedicated calendar spread analytics rather than as a technical rule.
